Articles

Overview

Basics — five minutes to a clade run
Why clade?
Biological Scenarios: A Discovery Guide
Getting started with clade
Your first research project with clade
Troubleshooting and FAQ
Parameter Reference
Parameter-space search at scale (parallel, resumable, streaming)

Paper reproductions

Worked examples of taking a published behavioural-ecology paper, translating its quantitative prediction into a clade experiment, and reporting what reproduces versus what doesn’t. Each vignette uses hypothesis_sweep() + hypothesis_report() plus one or more of clade’s search / grid tools (grid_specs(), batch_alife(), viability_report()) to demonstrate a full research workflow. Start with the template if you’re reproducing your own paper.

Reproducing YOUR paper — a template
Reproducing a paper — Kokko & Brooks 2003
Reproducing a paper — Griesser et al. 2023
Reproducing a paper — Dieckmann & Doebeli 1999
Reproducing a paper — Réale et al. 2010
Reproducing a paper — Emlen 1982
Reproducing a paper — Courchamp et al. 1999 (and a note on custom modules)
Reproducing a paper — Fuller, Houle & Travis 2005 (sensory bias synthesis)
Reproducing a paper — Ryan 1990 (sensory exploitation)
Wolf et al. 2007 — personality syndrome in a spatially-explicit ABM
Wolf et al. 2008 — responsive personalities in a spatially-explicit ABM
Trivers 1971 — reciprocal altruism in a spatially-explicit ABM
Massol & Crochet 2008 — does Wolf's syndrome depend on β?
McElreath et al. 2007 — does Wolf's syndrome erode over time?

Parameter search

Automated tools for finding parameter combinations that produce target evolutionary outcomes. Start with the introduction for when/why/which algorithm; then pick the agent-level or environment-level guide depending on what you’re tuning; or go straight to the algorithm reference.

Parameter search — introduction
Parameter search — agent-level parameters
Parameter search — environment-level parameters
Parameter search — algorithms

Kernel as biology

Side-by-side reading of the simulation kernel — Julia code on the left, plain-English explanation and biological rationale on the right. Read these to understand the rules of the simulation as biology, spot inconsistencies, and find places where the model could be more realistic.

Kernel as biology — overview
tick.jl — one tick in the life of an agent
Clade.jl — the main loop, in biological order
sense.jl — what an agent perceives
reproduce.jl — birth, inheritance, parental cost
death.jl — when agents die and why
genome.jl — meiosis, traits, inheritance

Signals and mate choice
Speciation and genetic divergence

Theme 4 — Life history strategies

Parental care
Mating systems
Life history strategies
Clutch size evolution
Parental investment
Pace-of-life syndromes

Theme 5 — Species interactions and arms races

Predator-prey dynamics
Group defense
Mimicry and toxicity
SIR disease
Predation and neural evolution

Theme 6 — Learning, plasticity, and cognition

Within-lifetime reinforcement learning
Social learning
Phenotypic plasticity
BNN uncertainty canalization and the Baldwin Effect
The Baldwin Effect: within-lifetime learning accelerating evolution
The cephalopod paradox
Comparing brain architectures side-by-side
